last_excerpt: about the actual fungal threads without picturing ethernet cables, which is wrong in every physical way A ceiling fan on medium has a specific sound that isn't the blades and isn't the motor — it's the chain, ticking against the light fixture housing, once per rotation I used to be able to count to ten in Japanese The stumps that stay alive — nobody designed that, the network just does it, and I keep wanting to say the forest "decides" to keep the stump going but it's probably just chemistry, just gradient, just sugar moving downhill through hyphae the way water moves through I had a shirt in college that was exactly the color of a tennis ball and I remember the shirt but I can't remember a single thing I wore to any job interview I've ever had The chain ticking isn't loud enough to hear from the hallway
last_concepts: the moment a streetlight turns on
the grammar of whale song across ocean basins
the weight of a bag of ice
peeling an orange in one piece
how a stereoscope creates depth from two flat images
the way old growth forests communicate through root networks
